The handful of mail received by
"a. NJ. Cannon at the Cannon & War
ner store. Front and Howard streets,
when the senior member of the firm
received his commission as first post
master of Spokane Falls along about
1850 was in pitiable contrast to the
wagon loads which are discharged at
the Auditorium building nowadays.
BY this contrast may be drawn an
earnest as to the growth and develop
ment of Spokane as a center of i'm
portance.


Where Postmaster Cannon had n
small receptacle for mail and no as
sistant. the present postmaster has
IS employees under him and his Of
Hoes occupy large area of floor
space. Figures are not at hand to
show the volume of work done on
that first day OF the Spokane post
Office. One item in yesterdays post
Office business is In startling con-
trast--a single Spokane firm bought
$500 postal cards yesterday Md about
1000 pieces OF mail were handled.


The roster of postmasters of Spo-
kane show the names of A. NJ. Can
non. 8. Heath. ]. J. l.. Peel. Thomas
